Schedules flexible work assignments and travel to London more frequently to provide ongoing support for her sister and newborn, even at the expense of turning down some freelance opportunities
Frequently seeks out baby-friendly cafés or quiet parks in London for outings with her sister and the newborn during visits, preferring these over bustling central city spots

Maintains connections with past clients by sending personalized photo postcards depicting Berlin or recent local work, as doctor-ordered travel restrictions prevent access to new international destinations
Explores street art and galleries exclusively in Berlin and the surrounding area, pausing cultural immersion in new cities due to medical travel restrictions
Uses her recovery period to connect with Berlin-based creative peers through coffee meetups and casual portfolio reviews, prioritizing in-person networking over remote collaborations
Begins documenting her recovery and local Berlin experiences through Instagram stories, shifting her content from travel focus to personal wellness and urban life updates

superseded_preferences.fact: Edits photos in late-night sessions after shoots — never delegates retouching to anyone else
superseded_preferences.reason: Her wrist is immobilized for several weeks; she cannot use a mouse or tablet for photo editing, so this personal editing routine is entirely infeasible until recovery.

superseded_preferences.fact: Schedules all freelance assignments to avoid overlapping travel dates, refusing double bookings
superseded_preferences.reason: The exclusive year-long contract requires her to prioritize this magazine's assignments over all other freelance work, and possibly accept overlapping or tighter scheduling to fulfill contractual obligations.

superseded_preferences.fact: Only accepts projects that allow creative control over both photography and location — refuses generic stock photo gigs
superseded_preferences.reason: The contract means she is obligated to execute assigned shoots which may not grant her previous level of creative control or freedom to choose locations.

superseded_preferences.fact: Adopts local cuisine when traveling, but never eats dairy or red meat when at home in Berlin
superseded_preferences.reason: After repeated hospitalizations abroad and a medical order, she can no longer freely adopt local cuisine if it contains the allergen. She must now always screen for this ingredient, sometimes refusing regional specialties that would have previously been a core part of her travel food experience.

superseded_preferences.fact: Packs lightweight resistance bands to do stretching exercises daily in hotel rooms — never misses, even on shoot days
superseded_preferences.reason: Her resistance bands were packed with the stolen camera equipment; she cannot use or pack them until replacements are arranged.

superseded_preferences.fact: Swims at least twice a week when in Berlin, choosing swimming over gym workouts whenever possible
superseded_preferences.reason: Her doctor advised to pause swimming and avoid strain due to lumbar disc herniation. To stay active and support recovery, she switches to low-impact, medically-approved activities.

superseded_preferences.fact: Video-calls her sister every Sunday evening, regardless of location — never switches to texting for the weekly check-in
superseded_preferences.reason: With her sister asking for in-person help after giving birth, the usual remote check-in pattern will be replaced by direct support and face-to-face time in London, making weekly video calls unnecessary or redundant while she is there.

superseded_preferences.fact: Checks in with parents monthly via a group video call, never lets more than six weeks pass without contact
superseded_preferences.reason: Her parents are relocating from Spain to an apartment near hers in Berlin, making in-person visits much more convenient and natural than scheduled video calls. The behavior shifts from remote check-ins to frequent, in-person family time.

superseded_preferences.fact: Keeps dating casual and short-term, never commits to long-distance relationships
superseded_preferences.reason: Preparing to be maid of honor means she is focused on supporting her sister and helping with wedding planning, and travel schedules complicate time for casual dating. She consciously decides to pause romantic pursuits in favor of her family role.

superseded_preferences.fact: Maintains connections with past clients by sending personalized photo postcards from new destinations
superseded_preferences.reason: The rabies exposure and mandatory vaccine protocol prohibit international travel for several weeks, meaning she cannot produce postcards from new foreign locations as she usually would; her connection efforts must focus on Berlin or other recent, local content.

superseded_preferences.fact: Explores local street art and galleries in every city she visits — never skips opportunities for cultural immersion
superseded_preferences.reason: Because her doctor has forbidden international travel while she completes her vaccine course, she cannot visit new cities and must limit her cultural outings to Berlin or local sites.

superseded_preferences.fact: Journals travel experiences nightly, handwriting rather than using electronic devices
superseded_preferences.reason: Her wrist fracture prevents handwriting and extensive use of electronic devices with one hand. She adopts voice memos as a direct substitute for nightly reflection and documentation.

superseded_preferences.fact: Uses downtime on shoots to walk and photograph neighborhoods just for fun, never schedules formal tourist excursions
superseded_preferences.reason: Extended walking for street photography is discouraged while herniated disc is healing; she needs physically restful downtime, shifting her off-tour custom from walks to indoor, sedentary activities.

superseded_preferences.fact: Backs up photo files nightly to two separate drives — never relies solely on cloud storage
superseded_preferences.reason: Her usual backup drives were in the stolen gear bag; while waiting for replacements she must adapt to using temporary rental gear and cloud storage to secure her work.

superseded_preferences.fact: Prioritizes assignments from the exclusive magazine contract, accepting overlapping travel dates when necessary for their shoots; only declines double bookings outside contract requirements
superseded_preferences.reason: Winning the prestigious international grant for a multi-month South America documentary series forces her to put all other assignments, including her magazine contract, on hold to focus full-time on the grant project.

superseded_preferences.fact: Sets aside two days per month to brainstorm and propose new feature ideas exclusively for the magazine, shifting focus from pitching to multiple outlets
superseded_preferences.reason: The grant’s exclusive scope and the ongoing collaborative production schedule mean she cannot reliably set aside time or creative resources for non-grant magazine pitches during the 4-month fieldwork period.

superseded_preferences.fact: No longer dates during the months leading up to her sister's wedding, prioritizing family commitments over casual relationships until the event is over
superseded_preferences.reason: The wedding planning and associated commitments are complete and her focus has shifted to supporting her sister post-birth rather than wedding preparations; the specific event (wedding) referenced by this preference is over.

superseded_preferences.fact: Coordinates her autumn schedule to be available in London for wedding rehearsals and planning activities, declining magazine assignments that conflict with wedding commitments
superseded_preferences.reason: The wedding and associated commitments are no longer ongoing and do not impact current work schedules; her focus now shifts fully to the grant project and South American fieldwork.

superseded_preferences.fact: Initiates frequent video calls with her sister mid-week (in addition to Sunday) to help with wedding preparations, favoring collaborative planning over their usual catch-up routine
superseded_preferences.reason: Wedding preparations with her sister are now complete and no longer require mid-week planning calls; the specific context for this preference has ended.

superseded_preferences.fact: Rents unfamiliar camera equipment for shoots and spends extra time learning new controls and settings before each assignment
superseded_preferences.reason: Her doctor recommends avoiding carrying and operating heavy camera gear; she is pausing hands-on use of professional equipment for at least two months and cannot meaningfully continue this gear-acclimation pattern.

superseded_preferences.fact: Delegates on-location camera setup, breakdown, and heavy gear transport to local assistants or fixers during assignments, never carries or lifts equipment herself due to medical restriction
superseded_preferences.reason: The wrist fracture makes her unable to perform or direct any camera operations herself, not just lifting or setup; active shooting is impossible, so she must either fully delegate all shooting or pause work assignments.

superseded_preferences.fact: Pre-contacts local hosts, fixers, or guides before each assignment to share her food restriction and request ingredient lists or safe recommendations before arrival
superseded_preferences.reason: She is medically prohibited from accepting new international assignments, so there are no upcoming trips requiring pre-contact with local guides or hosts; the logistical need for this behavior is suspended until travel resumes.
